author: lu_pinto
content: Do not want to pollute the chat, let me know what you think there

author: misilva73
content: Is your proposal to refund the EIP-7702 state gas to the place where that cost was taken from? instead of using the general researvoir refund?

author: misilva73
content: And aagree that this is the key issue of refunding to the reservoir - we are moving gas from a more general place (gas_left) to a more restrictive place (state_reservoir)

author: misilva73
content: So users may need to give larger gas limits to account for this

author: lu_pinto
content:
>Is your proposal to refund the EIP-7702 state gas to the place where that cost was taken from? instead of using the general researvoir refund?

yup! Basically I actually consider this an overcharge that is corrected shortly after rather than a refund. So I think giving it back where it was taken from makes more sense.

As you said if users give a larger gas limit enough to have the corresponding worst case 7702 authorization charges in the reservoir then this is side effect free.

author: misilva73
content: I see. I would love to hear thoughs on this from <@755913785620824175> and <@429719902363058177>. Also, since we have that proposal from <@402853454126776320> that is improving the refunds and charges for 7702, maybe we could fold these two things together?
